Ring of Winter  
 

Lore Item
Slot: FINGER
AC: 3
WIS: +5 INT: +5 HP: +55 MANA: +25
SV MAGIC: +5
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: CLR DRU SHM NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Ring of Winter
Lore Item
Slot: FINGER
AC: 3
WIS: +5 INT: +5 HP: +55 MANA: +25
SV MAGIC: +5
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: CLR DRU SHM NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Lore:Frostreaver's Insignia Ring
Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Tribute:1773
Submitted By:Flareon MacEevee
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-17 09:58:31
Page Updated:Sat Apr 20th, 2024

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: 1,383pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Icewell Keep
NPC Name
Dain Frostreaver IV
The Fabled Dain Frostreaver IV


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Icewell Keep


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
where's the fire
QuoteReply
# Apr 28 2004 at 2:36 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591804024399
View User Forum
27 posts
I can't understand why you all are making such a big deal about this ring? If you're powerful enough to take out the dain, you've got better. What a POS
▲ Page top
RE: where's the fire
QuoteReply
# May 24 2005 at 5:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362304782
5 posts
Maybe it's because their posts are years old...

I realize I'm commenting on an old post that comments on old posts....

But jesus, how stupid are you? When they were fussing over this ring, it was pretty damn awesome. This was one of the best buyable rings in its time, and not that bad compared to the nodrops of the time either. Pull your head out of your PoP and GoD geared *** and read the dates on Velious item posts.
▲ Page top
Nice ring
QuoteReply
# Mar 19 2003 at 6:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591785951727
Scholar
19 posts
Killed him yesterday in 5 mns with 40 people without any loss among raiders (average level 62). The fight is 'easy' when you got tanks with real aggro weapons. Just keep in mind that main meleers can be banished, which require a perfect tank switching to keep aggro focused on a single guy (if you fail, Dain will slain everyone around in 30 sec). Hit really fast and really hard and is immune to slow. No resist needed for fight either, it's pure melee.

I won this nice ring yesterday, this is really a very nice (and rare) upgrade :-) I only realised afterward it wasn't no drop, making it even more valuable. FYI, our guild killed Dain IV a dozen time and this is only the 2nd time i see the ring dropped.

Edited, Wed Mar 19 05:58:27 2003
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 08 2001 at 1:28 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) cleric ring
RE: best class
QuoteReply
# Feb 03 2002 at 12:06 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251064527
110 posts
Actually, the "best" class is whatever class that can wear it. It doesn't offer anything that Clerics need more than Druid, Shaman, Necro, Wizard, Mage or Enchanter need. It would benefit a Necro just the same as a Cleric, just the same as a Druid, etc. etc.
▲ Page top
RE: best class
QuoteReply
# Jan 06 2003 at 11:13 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780342538
22 posts
I agree. The best class that can wear it is whoever buys it and can use it. Besides what does it matter, if a necro wants it , a necro can buy it. If a druid wants it a druid can buy it. Necros and shammies are not the only ones who need hps/ mana, but then some people are too ignorant to realize this.

#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 30 2001 at 11:05 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Your an idiot, do the Velious patch, you just dont have the right model....
#REDACTED, Posted: May 07 2001 at 1:14 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If this item is really dropable and someone is selling on Mith Marr plz send me a tell! ;)

▲ Page top
RE: Dain Frostreaver IV
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 9:17 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The ring drops off of a level 70 dwarf...he has about 100,000-200,000 hitpoints, and hits from 35-425 dmg, hits EXTREMELY fast, and casts a DD. This guy takes about 15-20 min to kill with 70-80 people. Good luck trying to get the ring...if someone does get it, I would see no reason for them to sell it, more than likley it would go to someone else in the guild that kill the Dain.

P.S.
I have helped in killing the Dain, and he is one mean dwarf.
▲ Page top
RE: Dain Frostreaver IV
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2002 at 1:40 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758192590
17 posts
My guild just killed him tonight. Took us 15 minutes with about 40 people. I can't imagine trying to do it with 70-80, the lag would be incredible I would think.

I'd estimate his HP at approx 150k. Aggro is difficult to manage, he does indeed hit hard and fast. Poor FR though. MR seemed high.

He did drop this ring. If it were sold the price would be very high I would think, considering the rarity alone. I was surprised it was droppable at all, frankly. I agree it would be passed down in all liklihood.

Teneran
Sorcerer

#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 25 2001 at 7:12 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) for the druid epic i think we should have to give a box of firebeetle eyes to the druid guild leader in any town
#Anonymous, Posted: Jan 25 2001 at 12:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) hehehe just like verant make an item that is limited to uber guilds and stick it to us casual players.
RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2001 at 7:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756860139
87 posts
If you just play casually why do you need one?
they make these items hard to get to give you a goal to work toward. So the game doen't stagnate and get boreing. my goal is to go to the planes to get all this cool stuff for my druid, my guild is not that uber but our alliance is and we regularly schedual raids to the planes and veeshan. who ever says that uber guilds with hundreds of members controls the planes and nice items, is on crack. I have heard of good teams of players breaking into planes with as few as 6 peoeple, not clearing it mind you but breaking it and not just one spawn. It is posible for you to get these thing you just have to have people to help you. The whole idea behind this game and what verant is trying to ram down out throats is that you need to group to succede.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 25 2001 at 7:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If you just play casually why do you need one?
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 25 2001 at 7:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If you just play casually why do you need one?
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 25 2001 at 7:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If you just play casually why do you need one?
#Anonymous, Posted: Jan 25 2001 at 12:36 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Uuh, how exactly is Verant "sticking" it to you? And did you really expect it to be common loot from spiderlings?
#Anonymous, Posted: Jan 29 2001 at 2:30 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Verant is sticking it to the casual player because there are only basically 2 int items an enchanter has any real chance of getting. Both of these if done well are actually too easy to get, and I am speaking of the gold ear studs from the quest killing Cubert, and the Rodcet Nife Hoop which involves using a mule to kill the Koalindl fish. Other than those 2 items, there is virtually nothing that gives an int boost that is attainable without a strong group in Velious or an Uber group in one of the plains. Where are some middle value int boost items? Ones that aren't as high level as this ring, maybe +1 or +2 int with +5 mana. Ones that a more casual player has a chance at. Ones tht don't end up costing 2 to 20 kpp to be able to buy - casual players will never have that kind of extra pp. Yes, Verant is sticking it to casual players, and especially Enchanters, as pick up groups aren't as good as uber guild groups, and you will never see the kind of loot that you need to trade to acquire the equipment you really need.
RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Feb 04 2001 at 7:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593972048044
Scholar
147 posts
Umm, what crack have you been smoking? There are many +Int items available without velious/uber stuff...
A few examples (all relatively easy to get yourself or sell for under 1K, prices based on older servers):

Platinum Star Rose Quartz rings : +4 Int, sells for maybe 200pp

Flowing Black Robe : +5 Sta, +3 Int. Sells for a couple hundred, or can be aquired in Najena

Platinum Sapphire Necklace : +7 Str, +7, sells for around 500pp.

Stein of Moggok : +5 Dex +10 Int +25 vsDis +10 HP. Can be purchased for around 200pp, or aquired by a level 12 enchanter willing to put some time into running around the world doing the quest (takes a couple hours if you don't get someone to port you around).

There are MANY more... these were just the first 3 that came to mind. You want mid-range Int items, try looking around the dungeons on Antonica - NOT the new stuff coming from Velious dungeons.
▲ Page top
RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2001 at 5:30 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591755936927
73 posts
Provided you are responding to the post I think you are, the gentleman actually said there are no +5 intelligence rings other than what comes out of velious, he did not say there's no int gear out there. Just figured I'd point that out as none of the items you quoted were rings, and some of them were of less than +5 intelligence. Of course, you could have been responding to a post my score filter has skipped and as such I do not see it, in qhich case carry on, and ignore this.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jan 27 2001 at 1:48 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i actually agree with the first guy the game is way too much centered around group play why should the casual player who has a real life suffer from not being able to attain some good itemd and to say that casual players do not deserve good items??? the gam should be designed better to accomidate both types of players just because you might play more than then the next person does not make you more deserving than others they should impliment some solo only quests that will test your skills and lvl of expierance and reward you accordingly just my 2 cents worth :)
RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2001 at 4:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756920787
13 posts
The Quest he was talking about in the post before is a solo quest. It takes a little time and effort, and can be streched in to many actual days if nessasary. I have several quests started so that when i get my lvl 12 spell Bind Affinity so i can go and do the quest with relative ease. I work 8 hr shifts and clean my house, make my husband dinner and I get to play for about 3 hr a night, but I've managed to aquire so very good and interesting equiptment, and almost 40 pp, still in the bank. Sure those PP will be gone to my next spells, but I've aquired those 40 pp in about 3 days (9 hours of gaming). So really, if you actually try to work for something you can do it.


Sorry about the attitude, but well, he made me mad.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 15 2001 at 1:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) yer kinda a moron
RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Jan 28 2001 at 3:01 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I can take up the other side of the argument. If you play only a fourth of the time I do, why should you get the same equipment I do? It is one of the things that destroyed UO. They geared it to the casual gamer, leaving no challenge for the other gamers. I do not consider myself a powergamer and I am not in any guild, but if I spend more time playing, I do expect to have better equipment. If I only play half an hour per day, I do not expect to reach level 60, raid the planes on a daily basis or have the best possible equipment. I think your perspective is a bit off...

Beranar
Necromancer of the 22nd season
Bristlebane Server
▲ Page top
RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Jan 30 2001 at 3:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754860522
7 posts
I am a casual to moderate player. I believe that if they made good items easily accessable to someone that can solo the quest, a whole lot more people would have that item. It throws the rarity off by quite a bit. Think of it like this. It takes you 4 hours to obtain an item by yourself. Now, add 3 more people. It takes 1/4th as long to find that item. Add a guild, and anyone can get their uber weapon in a little under an hour. Scary isn't it?
▲ Page top
RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2001 at 12:02 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591752956671
2 posts
Well...I seriously doubt any guild can get an uber weapon in under an hour unless they get very lucky. A a guild(non-uber) we camp South Ro for the Ancient Cyclops. Nothing in the zone really causes any serious troubles, and we pretty much destroy anything there. Nonetheless, it is still an entire evening thing(3+hours) to get one ring for someone in the guild. damn fun time though!

Phaethen Zarathustra
21 Necromancer
Tribunal
Justice Keepers

RE: drops off Dain Frostreaver IV [nt]
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2001 at 5:38 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I agree. This ring is very nice. The only other 5int ring I know are the Velium player-made ones. And even those aren't made until you have a MASTER skill level of upwards of 200. So why should this ring be obtained by someone who doesn't have Master hunters in their party/guild? I guess what I'm saying is you have to be UBER good in jewelmaking so why not be UBER good in hunting to obtain a ring that rivals?

Just my 2cp

# Feb 15 2001 at 11:16 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Since everyone else is ranting about the subject. My *@%#&^* is about camp times. I can really understand the grouping/uber guild thing to get good stuff, but what about 10/12/20 hour spawns times. If you're going to make something challanging, make it difficult to kill or obtain. The challange shouldn't be to see if you can stomach sitting around for 12 hours waiting for a mob to pop. And then hoping he has the item. Whats the fun in that?
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 14 2001 at 11:06 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Well said good sir. They have all this land to build more spawn sites but no they would rather have a damm lion running around to attack you while your resting. Just remember folks.... EQ is a LARGE corporation they are all money sucking pigs!! they want you sit there for hours and hours and KEEP spending $10 a month.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jan 24 2001 at 10:38 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This must drop off something big and nasty.
